criteria.description: When merge is invoked without requesting a push, no network fetch against the remote repository shall be performed at any point during the merge operation.
criteria.notes: check_push_safety is gated by `if push:` in merge.py (WP02/T007). No-push path never enters that branch. Parametrized test verifies all 5 origin states.

criteria.description: When merge is invoked without requesting a push, the local merge shall proceed regardless of whether the local target branch is ahead, behind, diverged from, or in sync with its remote tracking branch.
criteria.notes: ATDD regression test for issue #1706 verifies local+ahead+behind does not block. Updated target_branch_preflight test confirms 'ahead' no longer blocks.

criteria.description: When merge is invoked with a push requested, the remote-state check shall fire only after all local lane integrations complete successfully, immediately before the push attempt.
criteria.evidence: tests/merge/test_push_preflight.py::test_check_push_safety_diverged_returns_not_safe + test_check_push_safety_ahead_returns_safe + test_check_push_safety_fetch_failure_returns_not_safe; call-site gate in src/specify_cli/cli/commands/merge.py (if push: ... check_push_safety)
criteria.notes: WP02/T007 gates _enforce_target_branch_sync_preflight with `if push:`. Push-safety check therefore only fires in the push path, after local merges.

criteria.description: When merge is invoked with a push requested and the local target branch is in a state that would cause a non-fast-forward or destructive push, the push shall be blocked with the existing diverged guidance and escape hatch. Local integration results shall be preserved.
criteria.notes: check_push_safety is read-only and does not mutate local git state. Test verifies is_safe_to_push=False for diverged and that sync_status (local state diagnostic) is preserved.

criteria.description: The sync-state value model shall distinguish between 'safe to merge locally' (true for all origin states) and 'safe to push' (false when the state would require a force push or cause destructive rewrite on origin).
criteria.notes: TargetBranchSyncStatus now has both is_safe_to_push (push-layer predicate) and is_safe (deprecated alias, always True). Parametrized test covers all 5 states.

criteria.description: The component responsible for performing remote-state inspection and network I/O against origin shall reside in the publish layer, not in the local-merge domain layer. The local-merge domain layer shall have no dependency on remote-state inspection.
criteria.evidence: src/specify_cli/merge/push_preflight.py (new publish-layer module with all remote-state types and check_push_safety); src/specify_cli/merge/preflight.py (re-exports only, no network I/O); architecture/3.x/adr/2026-06-05-1-merge-publish-layer-boundary.md
criteria.notes: WP01 created push_preflight.py. WP02 stripped network code from preflight.py and added re-exports. ADR documents the boundary decision.

criteria.description: The merge resume state shall persist whether the original invocation requested a push. A resumed merge shall respect the persisted push intent without requiring the user to re-supply the push flag.
criteria.evidence: src/specify_cli/merge/state.py: push_requested: bool = False field added; src/specify_cli/cli/commands/merge.py: MergeState(..., push_requested=push) at construction; tests/merge/test_merge_state_unit.py::test_from_dict (backwards compat via filter)
criteria.notes: WP02/T008 adds push_requested field with default False. merge.py passes push value at state construction. from_dict filters to known fields, so old state files load with default=False.

criteria.description: Existing merge resume state persisted without a push-intent field shall be readable without error, treating the absence of the field as 'push not requested.'
criteria.evidence: tests/merge/test_merge_state_unit.py::test_from_dict (verifies from_dict handles missing fields) + tests/merge/test_mission_number_idempotency.py::TestBackwardCompatibilityLoad::test_state_without_baked_flag_loads_as_false (parallel backwards-compat pattern)
criteria.notes: MergeState.from_dict filters to known fields before constructing dataclass. Absent push_requested field defaults to False. Existing test_from_dict covers this; no new test needed.

criteria.description: All tests that currently assert 'local ahead of origin' blocks the merge shall be updated to assert that condition does not block the merge.
criteria.notes: WP03/T009 inverted the blocked-ahead assertion. Test now uses diverged fixture (the only truly unsafe push state) instead of ahead (which is safe).

criteria.description: A regression test covering the exact scenario in issue #1706 (local target N commits ahead and M commits behind origin, merge requested without push) shall be added, asserting that the local merge completes without blocking.
criteria.notes: WP03/T011 adds regression tests for issue #1706. Both ATDD stub (WP01) and integration test (WP03) cover the exact diverged-but-no-push scenario.

criteria.description: Documentation advising users to use a PR-based workaround when local is ahead of origin during merge shall be removed and replaced with accurate guidance reflecting that local-ahead state does not block a local merge.
criteria.evidence: AGENTS.md: removed 'inspect before acting' / 'focused PR branch path' workaround block; replaced with accurate guidance: local merge is network-free, --push with diverged is blocked with remediation. CHANGELOG.md updated.
criteria.notes: WP04/T012 removed the outdated workaround from AGENTS.md. WP04/T013 added CHANGELOG.md entry referencing #1706.
